agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Telemedicine platform enabling patients to book virtual consultations with licensed physicians. The platform must enforce medical licensing compliance, scheduling integrity, access control over sensitive medical records, and auditability of consultation notes.","bounded_context":"Appointment Scheduling & Clinical Records","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Appointment","definition":"A scheduled time slot linking exactly one patient to exactly one licensed physician for a virtual consultation, with a defined start and end time."},{"term":"Licensed Physician","definition":"A medical professional whose license number has been verified by the platform and who is therefore eligible to accept patient bookings."},{"term":"Double-Booking","definition":"A scheduling violation where two or more appointments for the same physician have overlapping time ranges. This is a hard constraint that must never occur."},{"term":"Patient","definition":"An individual who books appointments, attends consultations, and owns their medical records."},{"term":"Staff Administrator","definition":"An internal platform operator with elevated privileges, including the ability to cancel appointments within the 2-hour pre-start window."},{"term":"Treating Physician","definition":"The specific physician assigned to an appointment; the only physician who may view that patient's medical records for that consultation."},{"term":"Consultation Notes","definition":"Clinical documentation created by the treating physician during or after an appointment. Becomes read-only (locked) 48 hours after the appointment ends."},{"term":"License Verification","definition":"The process of validating a physician's medical license number before the platform permits them to accept bookings."},{"term":"Cancellation Window","definition":"The period ending 2 hours before an appointment's start time, during which a patient may self-cancel. After this window closes, only a staff administrator may cancel."},{"term":"Medical Records","definition":"Protected health information belonging to a patient, accessible only to the patient themselves and their treating physician."},{"term":"Note Lock","definition":"The irreversible transition of consultation notes from editable to read-only state, triggered 48 hours after the appointment end time."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A patient can only book an appointment with a physician whose license has been verified.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Allowing an unlicensed physician to treat patients is a compliance and patient-safety violation. The system is in a corrupt state if this occurs.","testable_assertion":"For every appointment in the system, the associated physician's license_verified flag must be true at the time of booking."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"A physician cannot have two appointments with overlapping time ranges.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Double-booking violates physical reality — a physician cannot conduct two simultaneous consultations. If this occurs, the schedule is corrupt.","testable_assertion":"For any physician, no two appointments exist where appointment_A.start < appointment_B.end AND appointment_B.start < appointment_A.end."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"A patient may cancel their own appointment only if the current time is at least 2 hours before the appointment start time.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 2-hour threshold is a business policy that could be adjusted (e.g., to 1 hour or 4 hours) without corrupting system state.","testable_assertion":"A patient-initiated cancellation request is rejected when now >= (appointment.start_time - 2 hours)."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"A staff administrator may cancel any appointment at any time, including within the 2-hour pre-start window.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is an operational policy granting elevated privilege to administrators; the threshold and role could be adjusted by policy.","testable_assertion":"A cancellation request from a staff administrator succeeds regardless of how close the current time is to the appointment start time."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Patient medical records are visible only to the patient themselves and their treating physician.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Unauthorized access to medical records is a regulatory violation (e.g., HIPAA). If any other actor can read these records, the system's access control is broken.","testable_assertion":"Any query for a patient's medical records returns data only when the requester is the patient or the treating physician; all other requesters receive an access-denied response."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Consultation notes become read-only (locked) 48 hours after the appointment end time.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Once locked, clinical records must be immutable for legal and audit integrity. A system that allows edits to locked notes has corrupt audit history.","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to modify consultation notes where now >= (appointment.end_time + 48 hours) is rejected."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"A physician must have a verified license number before the platform allows them to accept any bookings.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is the gating precondition for BR-1. A physician in an unverified state accepting bookings represents a compliance-critical system corruption.","testable_assertion":"The system prevents creation of any appointment referencing a physician whose license_verified status is false or null."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A physician with a verified license and available time slot","when":"A patient attempts to book an appointment in that time slot","then":"The appointment is created successfully and the physician's schedule reflects the new booking"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A physician with an unverified or missing license number","when":"A patient attempts to book an appointment with that physician","then":"The booking is rejected with a clear error indicating the physician is not eligible for appointments"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A physician already has an appointment from 10:00 to 10:30","when":"A patient attempts to book that physician for 10:15 to 10:45","then":"The booking is rejected with an error indicating a scheduling conflict"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A physician has an appointment from 10:00 to 10:30","when":"A patient attempts to book that physician for 10:30 to 11:00","then":"The booking succeeds because the time ranges are adjacent, not overlapping"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A patient has an appointment starting in 3 hours","when":"The patient requests cancellation","then":"The appointment is cancelled successfully"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A patient has an appointment starting in 1 hour","when":"The patient requests cancellation","then":"The cancellation is rejected with a message indicating only a staff administrator can cancel at this point"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A patient has an appointment starting in 30 minutes","when":"A staff administrator requests cancellation of that appointment","then":"The appointment is cancelled successfully"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A patient with medical records linked to a treating physician","when":"The treating physician requests access to those records","then":"The records are returned successfully"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A patient with medical records linked to a treating physician","when":"A different physician (not the treating physician) requests access to those records","then":"Access is denied"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"A patient with medical records","when":"The patient themselves requests access","then":"The records are returned successfully"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"An appointment that ended 47 hours ago with editable consultation notes","when":"The treating physician edits the consultation notes","then":"The edit is saved successfully"},{"id":"AC-12","given":"An appointment that ended 49 hours ago","when":"The treating physician attempts to edit the consultation notes","then":"The edit is rejected and the notes remain in their locked read-only state"},{"id":"AC-13","given":"An appointment that ended exactly 48 hours ago","when":"The treating physician attempts to edit the consultation notes","then":"The edit is rejected because the 48-hour window has elapsed (boundary: >= 48 hours means locked)"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Patient attempts to book an appointment with a physician whose license was verified but has since expired or been revoked","expected_behavior":"The system should reject the booking. License verification must reflect current status, not just historical verification. A re-verification or status check mechanism is implied."},{"scenario":"Two patients simultaneously attempt to book the same physician for overlapping time slots","expected_behavior":"Exactly one booking succeeds; the other is rejected with a scheduling conflict. The system must enforce serialized conflict detection (e.g., optimistic locking or database-level constraint)."},{"scenario":"A patient tries to cancel at exactly the 2-hour boundary (now == appointment.start - 2 hours)","expected_behavior":"The cancellation should be rejected. The rule states 'up to 2 hours before,' meaning the patient must cancel strictly more than 2 hours before start time. At exactly 2 hours, the window has closed."},{"scenario":"Consultation notes are being actively edited when the 48-hour lock window arrives","expected_behavior":"The in-progress edit should be rejected upon save. The lock is based on wall-clock time at the moment of write, not at the moment editing began."},{"scenario":"A staff administrator who is also a physician tries to access records of a patient they are not treating","expected_behavior":"Access is denied. The administrator role grants cancellation privileges, not medical record access. Record access is strictly limited to the patient and their treating physician."},{"scenario":"A physician has their license revoked while they have future appointments","expected_behavior":"Existing future appointments should be flagged or cancelled. The system must not allow consultations with a physician whose license is no longer valid."},{"scenario":"Appointment is cancelled — what happens to the consultation notes?","expected_behavior":"If the consultation never occurred, no notes should exist. If notes were partially created, they should follow the same 48-hour lock rule from the original end time or cancellation time, depending on policy."},{"scenario":"Patient switches treating physician mid-consultation","expected_behavior":"Record access should transfer to the new treating physician and be revoked from the previous one. The previous physician should not retain access after reassignment."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Compliance violations (e.g., unlicensed physicians treating patients, unauthorized record access) carry significant regulatory fines. Double-booking wastes physician time and creates patient dissatisfaction leading to churn. Late cancellation policies protect physician revenue.","operational":"License verification is a gating workflow that must be reliable and potentially integrated with external licensing boards. The 48-hour note-lock window requires a reliable time-based job or check-on-access pattern. Overlap detection must handle concurrency under load.","user":"Patients expect seamless booking with confidence that their physician is qualified. Record privacy is a trust foundation — any breach erodes platform credibility. The cancellation window balances patient flexibility with physician schedule stability.","risk":"Primary risks are HIPAA/regulatory violations from improper record access, malpractice liability from unlicensed physician consultations, and data integrity risks from note-lock bypass. Each is a compliance-critical failure mode.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Patients","impact":"Booking confidence, cancellation flexibility, trust in record privacy, and assurance of physician qualifications."},{"group":"Physicians","impact":"Schedule integrity (no double-booking), ability to edit notes within the 48-hour window, and exclusive access to their patients' records."},{"group":"Staff Administrators","impact":"Ability to manage late cancellations and operational exceptions. Responsibility to act when patient self-service cannot."},{"group":"Compliance/Legal Team","impact":"License verification and record access controls are audit targets. Note immutability after lock supports legal defensibility."},{"group":"Platform Operator","impact":"Regulatory exposure from any invariant violation. Reputation risk from privacy breaches. Operational cost of license verification infrastructure."}]},"confidence":{"level":82,"reasoning":"The spec is clear, internally consistent, and covers the major domain rules explicitly. No contradictions detected. Deductions reflect minor areas where the spec is silent (license expiry lifecycle, record access transfer on physician change, exact boundary semantics) but these are normal edge cases rather than spec defects.","high_confidence_areas":["Double-booking prevention is unambiguous","Cancellation window rules are clearly defined with two distinct actor paths","Record access control is explicit: patient + treating physician only","Note lock timing is precise: 48 hours after appointment end","License verification as a booking precondition is clear"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether license verification is a one-time check or ongoing status — spec says 'verified' but doesn't address revocation lifecycle","Exact boundary behavior at the 2-hour cancellation cutoff (interpreted as strict inequality)","Whether consultation notes can exist for cancelled appointments","Record access behavior when a treating physician is reassigned","Whether the 48-hour lock is enforced on write or by a background job"],"assumptions":["The 2-hour cancellation window uses a strict inequality: patient can cancel only when now < (start - 2 hours)","The 48-hour note lock uses >= comparison: notes lock at exactly 48 hours post-end","License verification status is current, not just a historical one-time flag","Treating physician means the physician assigned to the specific appointment, not any physician the patient has ever seen","Staff administrator cancellation privilege does not extend to medical record access"]},"escalations":[],"rejection_reasons":[]}
